functions.name: NeuroVisKit._utils.lightning.pl_device_format
documentation:
Convert a torch device to the format expected by pytorch lightning.
**ONLY WORKS FOR SINGLE DEVICE**

Args:
    device: str or device object describing which device to use

Returns:
    str: the device formatted for pytorch lightning

functions.name: NeuroVisKit._utils.lightning.get_dirnames
documentation:
Returns a dictionary of directory names for the given config.
Args:
    config (dict): the config dictionary
Returns:
    dict: a dictionary of directory names

functions.name: NeuroVisKit._utils.lightning.prepare_dirs
documentation:
Creates the directories for the given config.

Args:
    config (dict): the config dictionary
Returns:
    dirs (dict): a dictionary of directory names
    config (dict): the config dictionary that has been updated with the session metadata.
    session (dict): the session dictionary containing metadata for the intended dataset.

classes.name: NeuroVisKit._utils.lightning.ThoroughModelCheckpoint
documentation:
A ModelCheckpoint that saves the entire model without dependencies.
**WARNING: may result in very large files.**

functions.name: NeuroVisKit._utils.postprocess.get_model_conv_weights

Get the weights of the convolutional layers of a model.
Returns a list of lists of weights.

functions.name: NeuroVisKit._utils.utils.reclass
documentation: Reclass an object to a new class

functions.name: NeuroVisKit._utils.utils.pad_causal
documentation:
Pad a tensor for a causal convolution.
x is a tensor
layer is a convolutional layer
kdims is the dimensions of the kernel that should be causal

functions.name: NeuroVisKit._utils.utils.KaimingTensor
documentation:
Generate a tensor with Kaiming uniform distribution.

Args:
    shape: shape of the tensor to be generated. 
    out_dim (int, optional): defaults to -1, used for determining the fan_out of the tensor.

functions.name: NeuroVisKit._utils.utils.extract_device
documentation: Get the device of a pytorch object.

functions.name: NeuroVisKit._utils.utils.getattr_recursive
documentation:
Get an attribute of an object recursively (e.g. obj.attr1.attr2.attr3)
obj: the object to get the attribute from
attr: the attribute to get (e.g. attr1.attr2.attr3)

functions.name: NeuroVisKit._utils.utils.uneven_tqdm
documentation: tqdm that works with uneven lengths of iterable and total

functions.name: NeuroVisKit._utils.utils.get_device_fancy
documentation:
Get a device from a string or device. If device is 'auto', it will choose the device with the most available memory.

Args:
    device (device|str): the device to get

Returns:
    device: the device

functions.name: NeuroVisKit._utils.utils.pad_to_shape
documentation:
Pads a tensor to a given shape.

Args:
    x is a tensor of any shape
    shape is a tuple of ints of the desired shape
    padding_mapper: a function to map the number of elements to pad to a list of padding values. Defaults to [0, p] which pads p elements to the right.

Returns:
    _type_: _description_

functions.name: NeuroVisKit._utils.utils.time_embedding
documentation:
Embeds a time series into a tensor of shape (time - num_lags, num_lags, n)

Args:
    x is (time, n)
    num_lags (int): number of lags to embed

Returns:
    (time - num_lags, num_lags, n)

functions.name: NeuroVisKit._utils.utils.get_module_dict
documentation:
Get a dictionary of all classes in a module that satisfy a condition.

Args:
    module (module): the module to get the classes from
    all_caps (bool, optional): whether to convert the keys to all caps. Defaults to False.
    condition (function, optional): a function that takes in a key and value and returns whether they should be included. Defaults to trivial condition.

Returns:
    dict: a dictionary of classes in the module that satisfy the condition

functions.name: NeuroVisKit._utils.utils.compose
documentation:
Compose single argument functions together, where the first function is applied first.

Args:
    *funcs: The functions to compose.

Returns:
    The composed function.

functions.name: NeuroVisKit._utils.utils.isInteractive
documentation: Check if the current session is interactive or not.

functions.name: NeuroVisKit._utils.utils.get_opt_dict
documentation:
Get the requested options from command line as a dictionary
opt_config: takes in a list of tuples, where each tuple is one of four options:
A. options that take an argument from the command line
    1. (shorthand:, fullname=, func) applies a function on the argument
    2. (shorthand:, fullname=) gives the argument as a string
B. options that do not take an argument from the command line
    3. (shorthand, fullname, value) sets argument to value if the option is present
    4. (shorthand, fullname) sets argument to True if the option is present

functions.name: NeuroVisKit._utils.utils.import_module_by_path
documentation:
Import a module by its path.

Args:
    module_path (str): the path to the module
    module_name (str, optional): the name of the module. Defaults to "custom_models".

Returns:
    module: the imported module

documentation:
This script tests the new regularization modules in foundation.utils.regularization

It compares them against the NDNT implementation

modules.name: NeuroVisKit.tests.tests
documentation: This script tests the new regularization modules in foundation.utils.regularization
It compares them against the NDNT implementation
X, Y, T = np.meshgrid(x, y, t)
w = w.permute(*[i for i in range(len(w.shape)) if i not in keepdims], *keepdims)
# layer = NDNLayer(input_dims = [1] + list(dim), filter_dims=[1] + list(dim), num_filters=NC)
def run_reg(ws, reg_loss, alpha=0.1, nsteps=100, verbose=True):
ws, grad, pen = step_reg(ws, reg_loss, alpha)
# inline lambda function that calls the regularization module
ws, grad = run_reg(ws0, reg_loss, alpha=0.001, nsteps=500)
ws, grad = run_reg(ws0, reg_loss, alpha=0.001, nsteps=500)
ws, grad = run_reg(ws0, reg_loss, alpha=0.1, nsteps=500)
ws, grad = run_reg(ws0, reg_loss, alpha=0.001, nsteps=500)
# regpen = reg.localConv(coefficient=1, target=ws, dims=[2,3], padding='same', padding_mode='constant', normalize=False)
# ws, grad = run_reg(ws0, reg_loss, alpha=0.001, nsteps=10)
#%% test that regularization doesn't scale with number of filters or upsampling
ws, grad = run_reg(ws0, reg_loss, alpha=0.001, nsteps=500)

functions.name: NeuroVisKit._utils.mess.get_sta
documentation:
Compute the STA for a given stimulus and response
stim: [N, C, H, W] tensor
robs: [N, NC] tensor
inds: indices to use for the analysis
modifier: function to apply to the stimulus before computing the STA
lags: list of lags to compute the STA over
time_reversed: if True, compute the effect of robs on future stim

returns: [NC, C, H, W, len(lags)] tensor

documentation: Script for generating a nice fitting pipeline.

variables.name: NeuroVisKit.minimal.device

variables.name: NeuroVisKit.minimal.model
documentation: optimizer defaults to Adam, LR defaults to 1e-3

Evaluate model on validation data.

Provide either a training dataset or a tensor of training firing rate means.

Evaluate model on validation data and plot histogram of bits/spike.

Provide either a training dataset or a tensor of training firing rate means.
Optional topk argument to only plot the best topk neurons.

functions.name: NeuroVisKit.utils.toy_datasets.get_center_surround_ds
documentation:
Generates a dataset of center-surround stimuli.
n: number of stimuli
size: size of each stimulus (size x size)
stds: shape (2, _) array of standard deviations for the center and surround

functions.name: NeuroVisKit.utils.mei.irfC
documentation:
Computes the IRFs for given neuron inds, time contiguous model and input

x (batched=False): input to the model shaped (num_lags, *spatial_dims)
x (batched=True): input to the model shaped (batch_size, num_lags, *spatial_dims)
model: model to compute IRFs for
neuron_inds: list of neuron indices to compute IRFs for

functions.name: NeuroVisKit.utils.mei.irf
documentation:
Computes the IRFs for given neuron inds, input, and traditional batched model.

Args:
    x (Tensor): input to the model shaped (batch_size, *any_shape)
    model (nn.Module): model to compute IRFs for.
    neuron_inds: list of neuron indices to compute IRFs for.

functions.name: NeuroVisKit.utils.utils.event_triggered_op
documentation:
Compute an event triggered operation (reverse correlation).

Args:
    covariate (Tensor): (time, *any_shape)
    events (Tensor): (time, <optional channels>) binary signal or (n_events,) indices
    range (tuple): (-time before event, time after event) non exclusive (i.e. arange(range[0], range[1]))
    inds (Tensor): Indices along time to include in computation. Defaults to None (i.e. include all data).
    reduction (function): Function to reduce the data along time axis. Must accept dim as keyword argument.

Returns:
    Tensor: (<optional channels>, range, *any_shape) where <optional channels> defaults to 1

functions.name: NeuroVisKit.utils.utils.r2

functions.name: NeuroVisKit.utils.utils.r2_numpy
documentation: Calculate the variance explained (r-squared) for numpy arrays.

functions.name: NeuroVisKit.utils.utils.r2_dfs
documentation:
calculate the variance explained (r-squared)
Inputs:

    yhat: tensor of shape N x NC

Outputs:
    r2: tensor of shape NC

methods.name: NeuroVisKit.utils.loss.LossWrapper.__init__
documentation:
Args:

    name (str): loss name.
    
    scalable (bool, optional): Whether loss gradients remain the same when number 
    of units is changed. Should be turned on whenever neurons dont have a shared core.
Wrap a loss module to allow added functionality of datafilters and proper scaling.

modules.name: NeuroVisKit.utils.rf
documentation: Collection of tools for analyzing and plotting RFs

functions.name: NeuroVisKit.utils.rf.get_sta
documentation:
Compute the STA for a given stimulus and response
stim: [N, C, H, W] tensor
robs: [N, NC] tensor
dfs: mask for included samples
inds: indices to use for the analysis
modifier: function to apply to the stimulus before computing the STA
lags: list of lags to compute the STA over

returns: [NC, C, H, W, len(lags)] tensor

functions.name: NeuroVisKit.utils.rf.minmax
documentation:
Normalize to be between 0 and 1
TODO: add support for dimensions

functions.name: NeuroVisKit.utils.rf.center_of_mass
documentation:
Compute the center of mass of a 1D, 2D, or 3D array
Input:
X: 1D, 2D, or 3D tensor
power: exponent to raise the values to before computing the center of mass (effectively a softmax)
thresh: threshold to apply to the values before computing the center of mass
        this removes noise from the center of mass calculation
Output:
    center of mass

functions.name: NeuroVisKit.utils.rf.get_spatial_power
documentation:
This function computes the spatial power of the STAs and returns the center

Inputs:
    spower - torch.tensor of size (width, height, num_neurons) containing the spatial power of the STAs
    sm - integer specifying the size of the spatial window to use for computing the spatial power
Outputs:
    spatial_power - torch.tensor of size (width, height, num_neurons) containing the spatial power of the STAs  
    ctr_x - float specifying the x-coordinate of the center of mass of the spatial power
    ctr_y - float specifying the y-coordinate of the center of mass of the spatial power

functions.name: NeuroVisKit.utils.rf.shift_stim
documentation:
This function samples a grid of size (size[0], size[1]) from the stimulus at the locations
specified by shift. The shift is in pixels and is a 2D vector. The output is a tensor of size
(stim.shape[0], stim.shape[1], size[0], size[1]) and the grid is returned as a tensor of size
(stim.shape[0], size[0], size[1], 2) so that it can be used with grid_sample (or diplayed for
animations that we should make to demo how this all works)

Inputs:
    stim: torch.tensor of size (stim.shape[0], stim.shape[1], stim.shape[2], stim.shape[3]) 
        where the first dimension is the batch dimension
    shift: torch.tensor of size (stim.shape[0], 2) where the second dimension is the x and y
        shift in pixels
    size: list of length 2 specifying the size of the output grid (in pixels)
    mode: string specifying the interpolation mode for grid_sample (default is bilinear)

Outputs:
    Image: torch.tensor of size (stim.shape[0], stim.shape[1], size[0], size[1]) where the first
        dimension is the batch dimension
    Grid: torch.tensor of size (stim.shape[0], size[0], size[1], 2) where the last dimension is
        the x and y coordinates of the grid

modules.name: NeuroVisKit.utils.plotting
documentation: this file should not have dependencies within this folder.

functions.name: NeuroVisKit.utils.plotting.plot_model_conv
documentation:
Plots the convolutional kernels of a model.

Recommended only for small models.

functions.name: NeuroVisKit.utils.plotting.plot_grid1d
documentation:
Plot a grid of figures such that each subfigure has m subplots.
mat is a list of lists of 1d data (n, m, x, p)
where p is the number of plots in each subplot
titles is a list of titles of length n*m

functions.name: NeuroVisKit.utils.plotting.plot_square_grid1d
documentation:
Plot a grid of n subplots.
mat is a list of lists of 1d data (n, x, p)
where p is the number of plots in each subplot
titles is a list of titles of length n

functions.name: NeuroVisKit.utils.plotting.plot_square_grid
documentation:
Plot a grid of figures such that each subfigure has m subplots.
mat is a list of lists of image data (n, m, x, y) or (n*m, x, y)
titles is a list of titles of length n*m

functions.name: NeuroVisKit.utils.plotting.plot_grid
documentation:
Plot a grid of figures such that each subfigure has m subplots.
mat is a list of lists of image data (n, m, x, y)
titles is a list of titles of length n.

functions.name: NeuroVisKit.utils.plotting.plot_split_grid
documentation:
Plot a grid of figures such that each subfigure has m subplots.
mat is a list of lists of image data (n, m, x, y)
titles is a list of titles of length n.
colors are split such that first half of m is red and second half is blue.
TODO probably move this to project specific repo.

functions.name: NeuroVisKit.utils.plotting.show_stim_movie
documentation:
Given stim show an interactive movie.

stim: (time, 1, x, y)

functions.name: NeuroVisKit.utils.datasets.BlockedDataLoader
documentation:
Creates a dataloader that returns contiguous blocks of data from a dataset.
Each block includes multiple samples, here "batch_size" operates on the block level and
the returned batches will NOT necessarily have the same size

classes.name: NeuroVisKit.utils.datasets.GenericDataset
documentation:
Generic Dataset can be used to create a quick pytorch dataset from a dictionary of tensors

Inputs:
    Data: Dictionary of tensors. Each key will be a covariate for the dataset.

classes.name: NeuroVisKit.utils.datasets.ContiguousDataset
documentation:
Contiguous Dataset creates a pytorch dataset from a dictionary of tensors that serves contiguous blocks
Called the same way as GenericDataset, but with an additional "blocks" argument

Inputs:
    Data: Dictionary of tensors. Each key will be a covariate for the dataset.
    Blocks: List of tuples. Each tuple is a start and stop index for a block of contiguous data.

classes.name: NeuroVisKit.utils.datasets.DSAutoDFS
documentation:
Dataset with automatic (and dynamic) data filter generation.

data is a dictionary of tensors.
blocks is a list of tuples, each tuple is a start and stop index for a block of contiguous data.
num_lags is the number of lags to filtered out in the beginning of each block.
min_blocksize is the minimum size of each block.

classes.name: NeuroVisKit.utils.regularization.Regularization
documentation:
Base class for regularization modules
Used for identifying regularization modules within a model.

methods.name: NeuroVisKit.utils.regularization.ProximalRegularizationModule.proximal
documentation:
Proximal operator -> apply proximal regularization to x
If easy, should return proximal gradients for visualization purposes

classes.name: NeuroVisKit.utils.regularization.Matrix
documentation: super class for applying a Matrix penalty to each targeted dimension

classes.name: NeuroVisKit.utils.regularization.max
documentation:
Dan's "max" penalty. Goal: sparsity
Matrix penalizes each weight element if any other weight is nonzero :
[0, 1, 1, 1],
[1, 0, 1, 1],
[1, 1, 0, 1],
[1, 1, 1, 0]
